# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that downloads build artifacts from external URLs provided as parameters and deploys them to production servers. This allows us to deploy pre-built artifacts from different sources (S3, Artifactory, HTTP servers) without rebuilding.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'ARTIFACT_URLS', defaultValue: 'https://github.com/cloudbees/cloudbees-cli/releases/download/2.37.0/cb-cli-2.37.0.zip\nhttps://example.com/some/other/artifact.tar.gz', description: 'Multiline list of artifact URLs to download. Each URL on a new line.')
        string(name: 'TARGET_SERVER_IP', defaultValue: '192.168.1.100', description: 'IP address or hostname of the production server.')
        string(name: 'TARGET_SERVER_USER', defaultValue: 'deployuser', description: 'Username for SSH access to the production server.')
        credentials(input: true, name: 'TARGET_SERVER_KEY_ID', credentialsType: 'SSH Username with private key', description: 'Jenkins SSH credential ID for the production server (username with private key).')
        string(name: 'DEPLOY_PATH', defaultValue: '/opt/myapp/releases', description: 'Base path on the target server where artifacts will be deployed.')
        booleanParam(name: 'CLEAN_DOWNLOADS', defaultValue: true, description: 'Clean up downloaded artifacts from Jenkins workspace after deployment.')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Initialize') {
            steps {
                script {
                    sh 'mkdir -p downloads' // Create a directory to store downloaded artifacts
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Download Artifacts') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def artifactUrls = ARTIFACT_URLS.split('\\n').findAll { it.trim() != '' }
                    if (artifactUrls.isEmpty()) {
                        error 'No artifact URLs provided. Please specify URLs in ARTIFACT_URLS parameter.'
                    }

                    artifactUrls.eachWithIndex { url, index ->
                        // Try to extract a sensible filename from the URL
                        def fileName = sh(script: "basename '${url}'", returnStdout: true).trim()
                        if (fileName.isEmpty() || fileName == '/' || fileName.contains('?')) {
                            // Fallback for URLs without clear filenames or with query strings
                            fileName = "artifact_${index}_" + UUID.randomUUID().toString().substring(0, 8)
                            echo "Could not determine clean filename from URL '${url}'. Using generic name: ${fileName}"
                        }
                        def localPath = "downloads/${fileName}"

                        echo "Downloading artifact from ${url} to ${localPath}"
                        // Using curl for downloading:
                        // -f: Fail silently (no HTML output on HTTP errors).
                        // -L: Follow redirects.
                        // -o: Write output to a local file.
                        // It's generally good practice to remove -k (insecure) unless absolutely necessary for specific test environments.
                        sh "curl -f -L -o ${localPath} '${url}'"
                        sh "ls -l ${localPath}" // Verify download
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Deploy Artifacts') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def downloadedFiles = sh(script: "ls -1 downloads/", returnStdout: true).trim().split('\\n').findAll { it.trim() != '' }
                    if (downloadedFiles.isEmpty()) {
                        error 'No artifacts were downloaded. Deployment cannot proceed.'
                    }

                    // Use sshagent to securely inject the SSH private key credential
                    sshagent(credentials: [TARGET_SERVER_KEY_ID]) {
                        echo "Connecting to ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP}"

                        // Create the deployment directory on the remote server if it doesn't exist
                        // -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no is used for convenience in CI/CD but consider managing known_hosts for production for better security.
                        sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'mkdir -p ${DEPLOY_PATH}'"

                        downloadedFiles.each { file ->
                            def localFilePath = "downloads/${file}"
                            def remoteFilePath = "${DEPLOY_PATH}/${file}"
                            echo "Transferring ${localFilePath} to ${TARGET_SERVER_IP}:${remoteFilePath}"
                            // Use scp to securely copy the file to the remote server
                            sh "scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${localFilePath} ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP}:${remoteFilePath}"
                        }

                        echo "All artifacts transferred to ${TARGET_SERVER_IP}:${DEPLOY_PATH}"

                        // === Start of Optional: Remote Post-Deployment Actions ===
                        // This section is highly application-specific. Examples below show how you might
                        // unpack an archive or restart a service on the target server.
                        echo "Executing optional post-deployment actions on ${TARGET_SERVER_IP}..."
                        // Example: List contents on the remote server
                        sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'ls -l ${DEPLOY_PATH}'"

                        // Example: Unpack the first downloaded artifact if it's a zip or tar.gz
                        /*
                        if (!downloadedFiles.isEmpty()) {
                            def firstArtifact = downloadedFiles[0]
                            def remoteFirstArtifactPath = "${DEPLOY_PATH}/${firstArtifact}"
                            def appContentPath = "${DEPLOY_PATH}/app_content" // Directory to extract into

                            sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'mkdir -p ${appContentPath}'"

                            if (firstArtifact.endsWith('.zip')) {
                                echo "Unpacking ${firstArtifact} on remote server..."
                                sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'unzip -o ${remoteFirstArtifactPath} -d ${appContentPath}'"
                            } else if (firstArtifact.endsWith('.tar.gz') || firstArtifact.endsWith('.tgz')) {
                                echo "Unpacking ${firstArtifact} on remote server..."
                                sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'tar -xzf ${remoteFirstArtifactPath} -C ${appContentPath}'"
                            }
                            // Add more file types as needed (e.g., .tar, .jar)
                        }
                        */

                        // Example: Restart a service on the remote server
                        // sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'sudo systemctl restart myapp.service'"

                        // Example: Create a symlink to the newly deployed release (e.g., if using a release directory structure)
                        // If you deploy 'myapp-v1.0.zip' into '/opt/myapp/releases/myapp-v1.0.zip'
                        // You might want to symlink '/opt/myapp/current' to the actual application directory within the release
                        /*
                        if (!downloadedFiles.isEmpty()) {
                           def firstArtifactName = downloadedFiles[0]
                           // Assuming the artifact unpacks into a directory of the same name or similar
                           def releaseDirName = firstArtifactName.substring(0, firstArtifactName.lastIndexOf('.')) // Remove extension
                           sh "ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no ${TARGET_SERVER_USER}@${TARGET_SERVER_IP} 'ln -sfn ${DEPLOY_PATH}/${releaseDirName} /opt/myapp/current'"
                        }
                        */
                        // === End of Optional: Remote Post-Deployment Actions ===
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Cleanup Local Downloads') {
            when { expression { CLEAN_DOWNLOADS == true } }
            steps {
                script {
                    echo 'Cleaning up downloaded artifacts from Jenkins workspace...'
                    sh 'rm -rf downloads'
                }
            }
        }
    }
    post {
        always {
            // Ensure the entire workspace is cleaned up after the pipeline finishes
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
